revision.comment: /* Arts and mythology */
revision.text:
{{wiktionary|Nara|nara}} '''Nara''' or '''Na-ra''' may refer to:
{{tocright}} == Places and jurisdictions == * [[Nara Prefecture]], a prefecture of Japan ** [[Nara (city)]], the capital city of Nara Prefecture, Japan * Nara, an ancient Roman city in Tunisia, now called [[Bir El Hafey]] * [[Nara, Attock]], a village in Attock, Pakistan * [[Nara, Jhelum]], a village in Jhelum, Pakistan * [[Nara Matore]], a village in Kahuta Tehsil, Rawalpindi District, Punjab, Pakistan * [[Nara, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa]], a Union Council of Abbottabad, Pakistan * [[Nara, Mali]], a town in Mali * [[Nara Basin]], a valley in Nara Prefecture, Japan * [[Nara Canal]], an excavated waterway in Sindh province, Pakistan * [[Nara Canal]], a paleochannel of the Indus and the Hakra, partly used by the Nara Canal * [[Nara (Oka)]], a river in Russia * [[Nara River (India)]], a river in Gujarat, India * [[Nara Burnu]], a cape in Turkey
== People and society == * [[Nara people]], a Nilotic ethnic minority inhabiting Eritrea * [[Nara language]], the mother tongue of the Nara people * [[Nara clan]], a Manchu clan in China * [[National Radical Camp]] or Nara Party, several Polish nationalist groups
== People with the name Nara == ===Given name=== * [[Nara Nath Acharya]] (1906–1988), Nepalese poet * [[Nara Apaya]], king of the Mrauk-U Dynasty of Arakan * [[Nara Bahadur Bista]], Nepalese politician * [[Nara Bahadur Dahal]] (born 1960), Nepalese long-distance runner * [[Nara Falcón]] (born 1981), Mexican synchronized swimmer * [[Jang Na-ra]] (born 1981), South Korean female singer * [[Nara Bahadur Karmacharya]], Nepalese politician * [[Nara Kollery]] (died 2015), Indian sound recordist * [[Kwon Nara]] (born 1991), South Korean female singer * [[Nara Leão]] (1942–1989), Brazilian female singer * [[Nara Lokesh]] (born 1983), Indian male politician * [[Nara Rohit]] (born 1984), Indian film actor and producer * [[Nara Bhupal Shah]] (1697–1743), king of the Gorkha state in Nepal
===Surname=== * [[Erika Nara]], Japanese Paralympic swimmer * [[Kurumi Nara]] (born 1991), Japanese tennis player * [[Rao Nara]] (1420–1487), crown Prince of Nadol, India *{{Nihongo|[[Setsuo Nara]]|奈良 節雄||born 1936}}, Japanese basketball player * [[Takeji Nara]] (1868–1962), general in the Imperial Japanese Army * [[Tatsuki Nara]] (born 1993), Japanese footballer * [[Tatunca Nara]] (born 1941), German-Brazilian jungle guide who invented the lost city of Akakor * [[Tōru Nara]], Japanese voice actor * [[Wanda Nara]] (born 1986), Argentine media personality and football agent * [[Yoshitomo Nara]] (born 1959), Japanese artist * [[Zaira Nara]] (born 1988), Argentine model and TV host * [[Empress Xiaojingxian|Ula Nara Duoqimuli]], Empress consort of Qing dynasty Yongzheng Emperor
== Arts and mythology == * [[Nara (Arjuna)]], Arjuna in his previous birth was Nara * ''[[Nara (album)]]'', by Emily Wurramara * [[Nara (comics)]], a fictional character from Marvel Comics * One of the twins [[Nara-Narayana]] in Hindu mythology * "Nara", song from the album ''Unearthed'' by [[E.S. Posthumus]] * [[Shikamaru Nara]], fictional character in the manga and anime series ''Naruto'' * "Nara", "Arrival In Nara" and "Leaving Nara", songs from the 2014 album ''[[This Is All Yours]]'' by alt-J
== Other uses == * [[Nara Hotel]], a hotel in Nara, Japan * [[Nara Station]], a railway station in Nara, Japan * [[Nara Television]], a Japanese television and radio broadcaster headquartered in Nara, Japan * [[Nara University]], a private university in Misasagi-cho, Nara, Japan * [[Nara Women's University]], a national university in Nara, Japan * [[Nara period]], period of Japanese history, named after the former capital city * [[Japanese ship Nara|Japanese ship ''Nara'']], {{Nihongo||楢 / なら||"oak"}}, several Japanese ships * [[Nara plant]] (''[[Acanthosicyos horridus]]''), a melon species of the Namib desert
== NARA as an acronym == '''NARA''' may stand for: * [[National Alien Registration Authority]], a defunct agency of the Government of Pakistan * [[National Amateur Rowing Association]], the governing body for rowing by working men in Britain prior to 1956 * [[National Archives and Records Administration]], an agency of the U.S. government * [[Neale Analysis of Reading Ability]], an assessment of comprehension and word reading accuracy
==See also== * [[Narra (disambiguation)]] * [[Naro (disambiguation)]] * [[Nari (disambiguation)]]
{{Disambiguation|geo|given name|surname}} [[Category:Language and nationality disambiguation pages]] [[Category:Japanese-language surnames]]

== People with the name Nara == ===Given name=== * [[Nara Nath Acharya]] (1906–1988), Nepalese poet * [[Nara Apaya]], king of the Mrauk-U Dynasty of Arakan * [[Nara Bahadur Bista]], Nepalese politician * [[Nara Bahadur Dahal]] (born 1960), Nepalese long-distance runner * [[Nara Falcón]] (born 1981), Mexican synchronized swimmer * [[Jang Na-ra]] (born 1981), South Korean female singer * [[Nara Bahadur Karmacharya]], Nepalese politician * [[Nara Kollery]] (died 2015), Indian sound recordist * [[Kwon Nara]] (born 1991), South Korean female singer * [[Nara Leão]] (1942–1989), Brazilian female singer * [[Nara Lokesh]] (born 1983), Indian male politician * [[Nara Rohit]] (born 1984), Indian film actor and producer * [[Nara Bhupal Shah]] (1697–1743), king of the Gorkha state in Nepal
===Surname=== * [[Erika Nara]], Japanese Paralympic swimmer * [[Kurumi Nara]] (born 1991), Japanese tennis player * [[Rao Nara]] (1420–1487), crown Prince of Nadol, India *{{Nihongo|[[Setsuo Nara]]|奈良 節雄||born 1936}}, Japanese basketball player * [[Takeji Nara]] (1868–1962), general in the Imperial Japanese Army * [[Tatsuki Nara]] (born 1993), Japanese footballer * [[Tatunca Nara]] (born 1941), German-Brazilian jungle guide who invented the lost city of Akakor * [[Tōru Nara]], Japanese voice actor * [[Wanda Nara]] (born 1986), Argentine media personality and football agent * [[Yoshitomo Nara]] (born 1959), Japanese artist * [[Zaira Nara]] (born 1988), Argentine model and TV host * [[Empress Xiaojingxian|Ula Nara Duoqimuli]], Empress consort of Qing dynasty Yongzheng Emperor

===Surname=== * [[Erika Nara]], Japanese Paralympic swimmer * [[Kurumi Nara]] (born 1991), Japanese tennis player * [[Rao Nara]] (1420–1487), crown Prince of Nadol, India *{{Nihongo|[[Setsuo Nara]]|奈良 節雄||born 1936}}, Japanese basketball player * [[Takeji Nara]] (1868–1962), general in the Imperial Japanese Army * [[Tatsuki Nara]] (born 1993), Japanese footballer * [[Tatunca Nara]] (born 1941), German-Brazilian jungle guide who invented the lost city of Akakor * [[Tōru Nara]], Japanese voice actor * [[Wanda Nara]] (born 1986), Argentine media personality and football agent * [[Yoshitomo Nara]] (born 1959), Japanese artist * [[Zaira Nara]] (born 1988), Argentine model and TV host * [[Empress Xiaojingxian|Ula Nara Duoqimuli]], Empress consort of Qing dynasty Yongzheng Emperor

{{wiktionary|Nara|nara}} '''Nara''' or '''Na-ra''' may refer to:
{{tocright}} == Places and jurisdictions == * [[Nara Prefecture]], a prefecture of Japan ** [[Nara (city)]], the capital city of Nara Prefecture, Japan * Nara, an ancient Roman city in Tunisia, now called [[Bir El Hafey]] * [[Nara, Attock]], a village in Attock, Pakistan * [[Nara, Jhelum]], a village in Jhelum, Pakistan * [[Nara Matore]], a village in Kahuta Tehsil, Rawalpindi District, Punjab, Pakistan * [[Nara, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa]], a Union Council of Abbottabad, Pakistan * [[Nara, Mali]], a town in Mali * [[Nara Basin]], a valley in Nara Prefecture, Japan * [[Nara Canal]], an excavated waterway in Sindh province, Pakistan * [[Nara Canal]], a paleochannel of the Indus and the Hakra, partly used by the Nara Canal * [[Nara (Oka)]], a river in Russia * [[Nara River (India)]], a river in Gujarat, India * [[Nara Burnu]], a cape in Turkey
== People and society == * [[Nara people]], a Nilotic ethnic minority inhabiting Eritrea * [[Nara language]], the mother tongue of the Nara people * [[Nara clan]], a Manchu clan in China * [[National Radical Camp]] or Nara Party, several Polish nationalist groups
== People with the name Nara == ===Given name=== * [[Nara Nath Acharya]] (1906–1988), Nepalese poet * [[Nara Apaya]], king of the Mrauk-U Dynasty of Arakan * [[Nara Bahadur Bista]], Nepalese politician * [[Nara Bahadur Dahal]] (born 1960), Nepalese long-distance runner * [[Nara Falcón]] (born 1981), Mexican synchronized swimmer * [[Jang Na-ra]] (born 1981), South Korean female singer * [[Nara Bahadur Karmacharya]], Nepalese politician * [[Nara Kollery]] (died 2015), Indian sound recordist * [[Kwon Nara]] (born 1991), South Korean female singer * [[Nara Leão]] (1942–1989), Brazilian female singer * [[Nara Lokesh]] (born 1983), Indian male politician * [[Nara Rohit]] (born 1984), Indian film actor and producer * [[Nara Bhupal Shah]] (1697–1743), king of the Gorkha state in Nepal
===Surname=== * [[Erika Nara]], Japanese Paralympic swimmer * [[Kurumi Nara]] (born 1991), Japanese tennis player * [[Rao Nara]] (1420–1487), crown Prince of Nadol, India *{{Nihongo|[[Setsuo Nara]]|奈良 節雄||born 1936}}, Japanese basketball player * [[Takeji Nara]] (1868–1962), general in the Imperial Japanese Army * [[Tatsuki Nara]] (born 1993), Japanese footballer * [[Tatunca Nara]] (born 1941), German-Brazilian jungle guide who invented the lost city of Akakor * [[Tōru Nara]], Japanese voice actor * [[Wanda Nara]] (born 1986), Argentine media personality and football agent * [[Yoshitomo Nara]] (born 1959), Japanese artist * [[Zaira Nara]] (born 1988), Argentine model and TV host * [[Empress Xiaojingxian|Ula Nara Duoqimuli]], Empress consort of Qing dynasty Yongzheng Emperor
== Arts and mythology == * [[Nara (Arjuna)]], Arjuna in his previous birth was Nara * ''[[Nara (album)]]'', by Emily Wurramara * [[Nara (comics)]], a fictional character from Marvel Comics * One of the twins [[Nara-Narayana]] in Hindu mythology * "Nara", song from the album ''Unearthed'' by [[E.S. Posthumus]] * [[Shikamaru Nara]], fictional character in the manga and anime series ''Naruto'' * "Nara", "Arrival In Nara" and "Leaving Nara", songs from the 2014 album ''[[This Is All Yours]]'' by alt-J
== Other uses == * [[Nara Hotel]], a hotel in Nara, Japan * [[Nara Station]], a railway station in Nara, Japan * [[Nara Television]], a Japanese television and radio broadcaster headquartered in Nara, Japan * [[Nara University]], a private university in Misasagi-cho, Nara, Japan * [[Nara Women's University]], a national university in Nara, Japan * [[Nara period]], period of Japanese history, named after the former capital city * [[Japanese ship Nara|Japanese ship ''Nara'']], {{Nihongo||楢 / なら||"oak"}}, several Japanese ships * [[Nara plant]] (''[[Acanthosicyos horridus]]''), a melon species of the Namib desert
== NARA as an acronym == '''NARA''' may stand for: * [[National Alien Registration Authority]], a defunct agency of the Government of Pakistan * [[National Amateur Rowing Association]], the governing body for rowing by working men in Britain prior to 1956 * [[National Archives and Records Administration]], an agency of the U.S. government * [[Neale Analysis of Reading Ability]], an assessment of comprehension and word reading accuracy

== Other uses == * [[Nara Hotel]], a hotel in Nara, Japan * [[Nara Station]], a railway station in Nara, Japan * [[Nara Television]], a Japanese television and radio broadcaster headquartered in Nara, Japan * [[Nara University]], a private university in Misasagi-cho, Nara, Japan * [[Nara Women's University]], a national university in Nara, Japan * [[Nara period]], period of Japanese history, named after the former capital city * [[Japanese ship Nara|Japanese ship ''Nara'']], {{Nihongo||楢 / なら||"oak"}}, several Japanese ships * [[Nara plant]] (''[[Acanthosicyos horridus]]''), a melon species of the Namib desert
== NARA as an acronym == '''NARA''' may stand for: * [[National Alien Registration Authority]], a defunct agency of the Government of Pakistan * [[National Amateur Rowing Association]], the governing body for rowing by working men in Britain prior to 1956 * [[National Archives and Records Administration]], an agency of the U.S. government * [[Neale Analysis of Reading Ability]], an assessment of comprehension and word reading accuracy

{{wiktionary|Nara|nara}} '''Nara''' or '''Na-ra''' may refer to:
{{tocright}} == Places and jurisdictions == * [[Nara Prefecture]], a prefecture of Japan ** [[Nara (city)]], the capital city of Nara Prefecture, Japan * Nara, an ancient Roman city in Tunisia, now called [[Bir El Hafey]] * [[Nara, Attock]], a village in Attock, Pakistan * [[Nara, Jhelum]], a village in Jhelum, Pakistan * [[Nara Matore]], a village in Kahuta Tehsil, Rawalpindi District, Punjab, Pakistan * [[Nara, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa]], a Union Council of Abbottabad, Pakistan * [[Nara, Mali]], a town in Mali * [[Nara Basin]], a valley in Nara Prefecture, Japan * [[Nara Canal]], an excavated waterway in Sindh province, Pakistan * [[Nara Canal]], a paleochannel of the Indus and the Hakra, partly used by the Nara Canal * [[Nara (Oka)]], a river in Russia * [[Nara River (India)]], a river in Gujarat, India * [[Nara Burnu]], a cape in Turkey
== People and society == * [[Nara people]], a Nilotic ethnic minority inhabiting Eritrea * [[Nara language]], the mother tongue of the Nara people * [[Nara clan]], a Manchu clan in China * [[National Radical Camp]] or Nara Party, several Polish nationalist groups
== People with the name Nara == ===Given name=== * [[Nara Nath Acharya]] (1906–1988), Nepalese poet * [[Nara Apaya]], king of the Mrauk-U Dynasty of Arakan * [[Nara Bahadur Bista]], Nepalese politician * [[Nara Bahadur Dahal]] (born 1960), Nepalese long-distance runner * [[Nara Falcón]] (born 1981), Mexican synchronized swimmer * [[Jang Na-ra]] (born 1981), South Korean female singer * [[Nara Bahadur Karmacharya]], Nepalese politician * [[Nara Kollery]] (died 2015), Indian sound recordist * [[Kwon Nara]] (born 1991), South Korean female singer * [[Nara Leão]] (1942–1989), Brazilian female singer * [[Nara Lokesh]] (born 1983), Indian male politician * [[Nara Rohit]] (born 1984), Indian film actor and producer * [[Nara Bhupal Shah]] (1697–1743), king of the Gorkha state in Nepal

{{wiktionary|Nara|nara}} '''Nara''' or '''Na-ra''' may refer to:
{{tocright}} == Places and jurisdictions == * [[Nara Prefecture]], a prefecture of Japan ** [[Nara (city)]], the capital city of Nara Prefecture, Japan * Nara, an ancient Roman city in Tunisia, now called [[Bir El Hafey]] * [[Nara, Attock]], a village in Attock, Pakistan * [[Nara, Jhelum]], a village in Jhelum, Pakistan * [[Nara Matore]], a village in Kahuta Tehsil, Rawalpindi District, Punjab, Pakistan * [[Nara, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa]], a Union Council of Abbottabad, Pakistan * [[Nara, Mali]], a town in Mali * [[Nara Basin]], a valley in Nara Prefecture, Japan * [[Nara Canal]], an excavated waterway in Sindh province, Pakistan * [[Nara Canal]], a paleochannel of the Indus and the Hakra, partly used by the Nara Canal * [[Nara (Oka)]], a river in Russia * [[Nara River (India)]], a river in Gujarat, India * [[Nara Burnu]], a cape in Turkey
== People and society == * [[Nara people]], a Nilotic ethnic minority inhabiting Eritrea * [[Nara language]], the mother tongue of the Nara people * [[Nara clan]], a Manchu clan in China * [[National Radical Camp]] or Nara Party, several Polish nationalist groups
== People with the name Nara == ===Given name=== * [[Nara Nath Acharya]] (1906–1988), Nepalese poet * [[Nara Apaya]], king of the Mrauk-U Dynasty of Arakan * [[Nara Bahadur Bista]], Nepalese politician * [[Nara Bahadur Dahal]] (born 1960), Nepalese long-distance runner * [[Nara Falcón]] (born 1981), Mexican synchronized swimmer * [[Jang Na-ra]] (born 1981), South Korean female singer * [[Nara Bahadur Karmacharya]], Nepalese politician * [[Nara Kollery]] (died 2015), Indian sound recordist * [[Kwon Nara]] (born 1991), South Korean female singer * [[Nara Leão]] (1942–1989), Brazilian female singer * [[Nara Lokesh]] (born 1983), Indian male politician * [[Nara Rohit]] (born 1984), Indian film actor and producer * [[Nara Bhupal Shah]] (1697–1743), king of the Gorkha state in Nepal
===Surname=== * [[Erika Nara]], Japanese Paralympic swimmer * [[Kurumi Nara]] (born 1991), Japanese tennis player * [[Rao Nara]] (1420–1487), crown Prince of Nadol, India *{{Nihongo|[[Setsuo Nara]]|奈良 節雄||born 1936}}, Japanese basketball player * [[Takeji Nara]] (1868–1962), general in the Imperial Japanese Army * [[Tatsuki Nara]] (born 1993), Japanese footballer * [[Tatunca Nara]] (born 1941), German-Brazilian jungle guide who invented the lost city of Akakor * [[Tōru Nara]], Japanese voice actor * [[Wanda Nara]] (born 1986), Argentine media personality and football agent * [[Yoshitomo Nara]] (born 1959), Japanese artist * [[Zaira Nara]] (born 1988), Argentine model and TV host * [[Empress Xiaojingxian|Ula Nara Duoqimuli]], Empress consort of Qing dynasty Yongzheng Emperor
== Arts and mythology == * [[Nara (Arjuna)]], Arjuna in his previous birth was Nara * ''[[Nara (album)]]'', by Emily Wurramara * [[Nara (comics)]], a fictional character from Marvel Comics * One of the twins [[Nara-Narayana]] in Hindu mythology * "Nara", song from the album ''Unearthed'' by [[E.S. Posthumus]] * [[Shikamaru Nara]], fictional character in the manga and anime series ''Naruto'' * "Nara", "Arrival In Nara" and "Leaving Nara", songs from the 2014 album ''[[This Is All Yours]]'' by alt-J
== Other uses == * [[Nara Hotel]], a hotel in Nara, Japan * [[Nara Station]], a railway station in Nara, Japan * [[Nara Television]], a Japanese television and radio broadcaster headquartered in Nara, Japan * [[Nara University]], a private university in Misasagi-cho, Nara, Japan * [[Nara Women's University]], a national university in Nara, Japan * [[Nara period]], period of Japanese history, named after the former capital city * [[Japanese ship Nara|Japanese ship ''Nara'']], {{Nihongo||楢 / なら||"oak"}}, several Japanese ships * [[Nara plant]] (''[[Acanthosicyos horridus]]''), a melon species of the Namib desert
== NARA as an acronym == '''NARA''' may stand for: * [[National Alien Registration Authority]], a defunct agency of the Government of Pakistan * [[National Amateur Rowing Association]], the governing body for rowing by working men in Britain prior to 1956 * [[National Archives and Records Administration]], an agency of the U.S. government * [[Neale Analysis of Reading Ability]], an assessment of comprehension and word reading accuracy
==See also== * [[Narra (disambiguation)]] * [[Naro (disambiguation)]] * [[Nari (disambiguation)]]

{{wiktionary|Nara|nara}} '''Nara''' or '''Na-ra''' may refer to:
{{tocright}} == Places and jurisdictions == * [[Nara Prefecture]], a prefecture of Japan ** [[Nara (city)]], the capital city of Nara Prefecture, Japan * Nara, an ancient Roman city in Tunisia, now called [[Bir El Hafey]] * [[Nara, Attock]], a village in Attock, Pakistan * [[Nara, Jhelum]], a village in Jhelum, Pakistan * [[Nara Matore]], a village in Kahuta Tehsil, Rawalpindi District, Punjab, Pakistan * [[Nara, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa]], a Union Council of Abbottabad, Pakistan * [[Nara, Mali]], a town in Mali * [[Nara Basin]], a valley in Nara Prefecture, Japan * [[Nara Canal]], an excavated waterway in Sindh province, Pakistan * [[Nara Canal]], a paleochannel of the Indus and the Hakra, partly used by the Nara Canal * [[Nara (Oka)]], a river in Russia * [[Nara River (India)]], a river in Gujarat, India * [[Nara Burnu]], a cape in Turkey
== People and society == * [[Nara people]], a Nilotic ethnic minority inhabiting Eritrea * [[Nara language]], the mother tongue of the Nara people * [[Nara clan]], a Manchu clan in China * [[National Radical Camp]] or Nara Party, several Polish nationalist groups
== People with the name Nara == ===Given name=== * [[Nara Nath Acharya]] (1906–1988), Nepalese poet * [[Nara Apaya]], king of the Mrauk-U Dynasty of Arakan * [[Nara Bahadur Bista]], Nepalese politician * [[Nara Bahadur Dahal]] (born 1960), Nepalese long-distance runner * [[Nara Falcón]] (born 1981), Mexican synchronized swimmer * [[Jang Na-ra]] (born 1981), South Korean female singer * [[Nara Bahadur Karmacharya]], Nepalese politician * [[Nara Kollery]] (died 2015), Indian sound recordist * [[Kwon Nara]] (born 1991), South Korean female singer * [[Nara Leão]] (1942–1989), Brazilian female singer * [[Nara Lokesh]] (born 1983), Indian male politician * [[Nara Rohit]] (born 1984), Indian film actor and producer * [[Nara Bhupal Shah]] (1697–1743), king of the Gorkha state in Nepal
===Surname=== * [[Erika Nara]], Japanese Paralympic swimmer * [[Kurumi Nara]] (born 1991), Japanese tennis player * [[Rao Nara]] (1420–1487), crown Prince of Nadol, India *{{Nihongo|[[Setsuo Nara]]|奈良 節雄||born 1936}}, Japanese basketball player * [[Takeji Nara]] (1868–1962), general in the Imperial Japanese Army * [[Tatsuki Nara]] (born 1993), Japanese footballer * [[Tatunca Nara]] (born 1941), German-Brazilian jungle guide who invented the lost city of Akakor * [[Tōru Nara]], Japanese voice actor * [[Wanda Nara]] (born 1986), Argentine media personality and football agent * [[Yoshitomo Nara]] (born 1959), Japanese artist * [[Zaira Nara]] (born 1988), Argentine model and TV host * [[Empress Xiaojingxian|Ula Nara Duoqimuli]], Empress consort of Qing dynasty Yongzheng Emperor
== Arts and mythology == * [[Nara (Arjuna)]], Arjuna in his previous birth was Nara * ''[[Nara (album)]]'', by Emily Wurramara * [[Nara (comics)]], a fictional character from Marvel Comics * One of the twins [[Nara-Narayana]] in Hindu mythology * "Nara", song from the album ''Unearthed'' by [[E.S. Posthumus]] * [[Shikamaru Nara]], fictional character in the manga and anime series ''Naruto'' * "Nara", "Arrival In Nara" and "Leaving Nara", songs from the 2014 album ''[[This Is All Yours]]'' by alt-J
== Other uses == * [[Nara Hotel]], a hotel in Nara, Japan * [[Nara Station]], a railway station in Nara, Japan * [[Nara Television]], a Japanese television and radio broadcaster headquartered in Nara, Japan * [[Nara University]], a private university in Misasagi-cho, Nara, Japan * [[Nara Women's University]], a national university in Nara, Japan * [[Nara period]], period of Japanese history, named after the former capital city * [[Japanese ship Nara|Japanese ship ''Nara'']], {{Nihongo||楢 / なら||"oak"}}, several Japanese ships * [[Nara plant]] (''[[Acanthosicyos horridus]]''), a melon species of the Namib desert
== NARA as an acronym == '''NARA''' may stand for: * [[National Alien Registration Authority]], a defunct agency of the Government of Pakistan * [[National Amateur Rowing Association]], the governing body for rowing by working men in Britain prior to 1956 * [[National Archives and Records Administration]], an agency of the U.S. government * [[Neale Analysis of Reading Ability]], an assessment of comprehension and word reading accuracy
==See also== * [[Narra (disambiguation)]] * [[Naro (disambiguation)]] * [[Nari (disambiguation)]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "category:" to "Category:"
revision.text:
{{wiktionary|Nara|nara}} '''Nara''' or '''Na-ra''' may refer to:
{{tocright}} == Places and jurisdictions == * [[Nara Prefecture]], a prefecture of Japan ** [[Nara (city)]], the capital city of Nara Prefecture, Japan * Nara, an ancient Roman city in Tunisia, now called [[Bir El Hafey]] * [[Nara, Attock]], a village in Attock, Pakistan * [[Nara, Jhelum]], a village in Jhelum, Pakistan * [[Nara Matore]], a village in Kahuta Tehsil, Rawalpindi District, Punjab, Pakistan * [[Nara, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa]], a Union Council of Abbottabad, Pakistan * [[Nara, Mali]], a town in Mali * [[Nara Basin]], a valley in Nara Prefecture, Japan * [[Nara Canal]], an excavated waterway in Sindh province, Pakistan * [[Nara Canal]], a paleochannel of the Indus and the Hakra, partly used by the Nara Canal * [[Nara (Oka)]], a river in Russia * [[Nara River (India)]], a river in Gujarat, India * [[Nara Burnu]], a cape in Turkey
== People and society == * [[Nara people]], a Nilotic ethnic minority inhabiting Eritrea * [[Nara language]], the mother tongue of the Nara people * [[Nara clan]], a Manchu clan in China * [[National Radical Camp]] or Nara Party, several Polish nationalist groups
== People with the name Nara == ===Given name=== * [[Nara Nath Acharya]] (1906–1988), Nepalese poet * [[Nara Apaya]], king of the Mrauk-U Dynasty of Arakan * [[Nara Bahadur Bista]], Nepalese politician * [[Nara Bahadur Dahal]] (born 1960), Nepalese long-distance runner * [[Nara Falcón]] (born 1981), Mexican synchronized swimmer * [[Jang Na-ra]] (born 1981), South Korean female singer * [[Nara Bahadur Karmacharya]], Nepalese politician * [[Nara Kollery]] (died 2015), Indian sound recordist * [[Kwon Nara]] (born 1991), South Korean female singer * [[Nara Leão]] (1942–1989), Brazilian female singer * [[Nara Lokesh]] (born 1983), Indian male politician * [[Nara Rohit]] (born 1984), Indian film actor and producer * [[Nara Bhupal Shah]] (1697–1743), king of the Gorkha state in Nepal
===Surname=== * [[Erika Nara]], Japanese Paralympic swimmer * [[Kurumi Nara]] (born 1991), Japanese tennis player * [[Rao Nara]] (1420–1487), crown Prince of Nadol, India *{{Nihongo|[[Setsuo Nara]]|奈良 節雄||born 1936}}, Japanese basketball player * [[Takeji Nara]] (1868–1962), general in the Imperial Japanese Army * [[Tatsuki Nara]] (born 1993), Japanese footballer * [[Tatunca Nara]] (born 1941), German-Brazilian jungle guide who invented the lost city of Akakor * [[Tōru Nara]], Japanese voice actor * [[Wanda Nara]] (born 1986), Argentine media personality and football agent * [[Yoshitomo Nara]] (born 1959), Japanese artist * [[Zaira Nara]] (born 1988), Argentine model and TV host * [[Empress Xiaojingxian|Ula Nara Duoqimuli]], Empress consort of Qing dynasty Yongzheng Emperor
== Arts and mythology == * [[Nara (Arjuna)]], Arjuna in his previous birth was Nara * ''[[Nara (album)]]'', by Emily Wurramara * [[Nara (comics)]], a fictional character from Marvel Comics * One of the twins [[Nara-Narayana]] in Hindu mythology * "Nara", song from the album ''Unearthed'' by [[E.S. Posthumus]] * [[Shikamaru Nara]], fictional character in the manga and anime series ''Naruto'' * "Nara", "Arrival In Nara" and "Leaving Nara", songs from the 2014 album ''[[This Is All Yours]]'' by alt-J
== Other uses == * [[Nara Hotel]], a hotel in Nara, Japan * [[Nara Station]], a railway station in Nara, Japan * [[Nara Television]], a Japanese television and radio broadcaster headquartered in Nara, Japan * [[Nara University]], a private university in Misasagi-cho, Nara, Japan * [[Nara Women's University]], a national university in Nara, Japan * [[Nara period]], period of Japanese history, named after the former capital city * [[Japanese ship Nara|Japanese ship ''Nara'']], {{Nihongo||楢 / なら||"oak"}}, several Japanese ships * [[Nara plant]] (''[[Acanthosicyos horridus]]''), a melon species of the Namib desert
== NARA as an acronym == '''NARA''' may stand for: * [[National Alien Registration Authority]], a defunct agency of the Government of Pakistan * [[National Amateur Rowing Association]], the governing body for rowing by working men in Britain prior to 1956 * [[National Archives and Records Administration]], an agency of the U.S. government * [[Neale Analysis of Reading Ability]], an assessment of comprehension and word reading accuracy
==See also== * [[Narra (disambiguation)]] * [[Naro (disambiguation)]] * [[Nari (disambiguation)]]

revision.text:
{{wiktionary|Nara|nara}} '''Nara''' or '''Na-ra''' may refer to:
{{tocright}} == Places and jurisdictions == * [[Nara Prefecture]], a prefecture of Japan ** [[Nara (city)]], the capital city of Nara Prefecture, Japan * Nara, an ancient Roman city in Tunisia, now called [[Bir El Hafey]] * [[Nara, Attock]], a village in Attock, Pakistan * [[Nara, Jhelum]], a village in Jhelum, Pakistan * [[Nara Matore]], a village in Kahuta Tehsil, Rawalpindi District, Punjab, Pakistan * [[Nara, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa]], a Union Council of Abbottabad, Pakistan * [[Nara, Mali]], a town in Mali * [[Nara Basin]], a valley in Nara Prefecture, Japan * [[Nara Canal]], an excavated waterway in Sindh province, Pakistan * [[Nara Canal]], a paleochannel of the Indus and the Hakra, partly used by the Nara Canal * [[Nara (Oka)]], a river in Russia * [[Nara River (India)]], a river in Gujarat, India * [[Nara Burnu]], a cape in Turkey
== People and society == * [[Nara people]], a Nilotic ethnic minority inhabiting Eritrea * [[Nara language]], the mother tongue of the Nara people * [[Nara clan]], a Manchu clan in China * [[National Radical Camp]] or Nara Party, several Polish nationalist groups
== People with the name Nara == ===Given name=== * [[Nara Nath Acharya]] (1906–1988), Nepalese poet * [[Nara Apaya]], king of the Mrauk-U Dynasty of Arakan * [[Nara Bahadur Bista]], Nepalese politician * [[Nara Bahadur Dahal]] (born 1960), Nepalese long-distance runner * [[Nara Falcón]] (born 1981), Mexican synchronized swimmer * [[Jang Na-ra]] (born 1981), South Korean female singer * [[Nara Bahadur Karmacharya]], Nepalese politician * [[Nara Kollery]] (died 2015), Indian sound recordist * [[Kwon Nara]] (born 1991), South Korean female singer * [[Nara Leão]] (1942–1989), Brazilian female singer * [[Nara Lokesh]] (born 1983), Indian male politician * [[Nara Rohit]] (born 1984), Indian film actor and producer * [[Nara Bhupal Shah]] (1697–1743), king of the Gorkha state in Nepal
===Surname=== * [[Erika Nara]], Japanese Paralympic swimmer * [[Kurumi Nara]] (born 1991), Japanese tennis player * [[Rao Nara]] (1420–1487), crown Prince of Nadol, India *{{Nihongo|[[Setsuo Nara]]|奈良 節雄||born 1936}}, Japanese basketball player * [[Takeji Nara]] (1868–1962), general in the Imperial Japanese Army * [[Tatsuki Nara]] (born 1993), Japanese footballer * [[Tatunca Nara]] (born 1941), German-Brazilian jungle guide who invented the lost city of Akakor * [[Tōru Nara]], Japanese voice actor * [[Wanda Nara]] (born 1986), Argentine media personality and football agent * [[Yoshitomo Nara]] (born 1959), Japanese artist * [[Zaira Nara]] (born 1988), Argentine model and TV host * [[Empress Xiaojingxian|Ula Nara Duoqimuli]], Empress consort of Qing dynasty Yongzheng Emperor
== Arts and mythology == * [[Nara (Arjuna)]], Arjuna in his previous birth was Nara * ''[[Nara (album)]]'', by Emily Wurramara * [[Nara (comics)]], a fictional character from Marvel Comics * One of the twins [[Nara-Narayana]] in Hindu mythology * "Nara", song from the album ''Unearthed'' by [[E.S. Posthumus]] * [[Shikamaru Nara]], fictional character in the manga and anime series ''Naruto'' * "Nara", "Arrival In Nara" and "Leaving Nara", songs from the 2014 album ''[[This Is All Yours]]'' by alt-J
== Other uses == * [[Nara Hotel]], a hotel in Nara, Japan * [[Nara Station]], a railway station in Nara, Japan * [[Nara Television]], a Japanese television and radio broadcaster headquartered in Nara, Japan * [[Nara University]], a private university in Misasagi-cho, Nara, Japan * [[Nara Women's University]], a national university in Nara, Japan * [[Nara period]], period of Japanese history, named after the former capital city * [[Japanese ship Nara|Japanese ship ''Nara'']], {{Nihongo||楢 / なら||"oak"}}, several Japanese ships * [[Nara plant]] (''[[Acanthosicyos horridus]]''), a melon species of the Namib desert
== NARA as an acronym == '''NARA''' may stand for: * [[National Alien Registration Authority]], a defunct agency of the Government of Pakistan * [[National Amateur Rowing Association]], the governing body for rowing by working men in Britain prior to 1956 * [[National Archives and Records Administration]], an agency of the U.S. government * [[Neale Analysis of Reading Ability]], an assessment of comprehension and word reading accuracy
==See also== * [[Narra (disambiguation)]] * [[Naro (disambiguation)]] * [[Nari (disambiguation)]]
{{Disambiguation|geo|given name|surname}}
<!--Nara--> <!--Nara-->

revision.text:
{{wiktionary|Nara|nara}} '''Nara''' or '''Na-ra''' may refer to:
{{tocright}} == Places and jurisdictions == * [[Nara Prefecture]], a prefecture of Japan ** [[Nara (city)]], the capital city of Nara Prefecture, Japan * Nara, an ancient Roman city in Tunisia, now called [[Bir El Hafey]] * [[Nara, Attock]], a village in Attock, Pakistan * [[Nara, Jhelum]], a village in Jhelum, Pakistan * [[Nara Matore]], a village in Kahuta Tehsil, Rawalpindi District, Punjab, Pakistan * [[Nara, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa]], a Union Council of Abbottabad, Pakistan * [[Nara, Mali]], a town in Mali * [[Nara Basin]], a valley in Nara Prefecture, Japan * [[Nara Canal]], an excavated waterway in Sindh province, Pakistan * [[Nara Canal]], a paleochannel of the Indus and the Hakra, partly used by the Nara Canal * [[Nara (Oka)]], a river in Russia * [[Nara River (India)]], a river in Gujarat, India * [[Nara Burnu]], a cape in Turkey
== People and society == * [[Nara people]], a Nilotic ethnic minority inhabiting Eritrea * [[Nara language]], the mother tongue of the Nara people * [[Nara clan]], a Manchu clan in China * [[National Radical Camp]] or Nara Party, several Polish nationalist groups
== People with the name Nara == ===Given name=== * [[Nara Nath Acharya]] (1906–1988), Nepalese poet * [[Nara Apaya]], king of the Mrauk-U Dynasty of Arakan * [[Nara Bahadur Bista]], Nepalese politician * [[Nara Bahadur Dahal]] (born 1960), Nepalese long-distance runner * [[Nara Falcón]] (born 1981), Mexican synchronized swimmer * [[Jang Na-ra]] (born 1981), South Korean female singer * [[Nara Bahadur Karmacharya]], Nepalese politician * [[Nara Kollery]] (died 2015), Indian sound recordist * [[Kwon Nara]] (born 1991), South Korean female singer * [[Nara Leão]] (1942–1989), Brazilian female singer * [[Nara Lokesh]] (born 1983), Indian male politician * [[Nara Rohit]] (born 1984), Indian film actor and producer * [[Nara Bhupal Shah]] (1697–1743), king of the Gorkha state in Nepal
===Surname=== * [[Erika Nara]], Japanese Paralympic swimmer * [[Kurumi Nara]] (born 1991), Japanese tennis player * [[Rao Nara]] (1420–1487), crown Prince of Nadol, India *{{Nihongo|[[Setsuo Nara]]|奈良 節雄||born 1936}}, Japanese basketball player * [[Takeji Nara]] (1868–1962), general in the Imperial Japanese Army * [[Tatsuki Nara]] (born 1993), Japanese footballer * [[Tatunca Nara]] (born 1941), German-Brazilian jungle guide who invented the lost city of Akakor * [[Tōru Nara]], Japanese voice actor * [[Wanda Nara]] (born 1986), Argentine media personality and football agent * [[Yoshitomo Nara]] (born 1959), Japanese artist * [[Zaira Nara]] (born 1988), Argentine model and TV host * [[Empress Xiaojingxian|Ula Nara Duoqimuli]], Empress consort of Qing dynasty Yongzheng Emperor
== Arts and mythology == * [[Nara (Arjuna)]], Arjuna in his previous birth was Nara * ''[[Nara (album)]]'', by Emily Wurramara * [[Nara (comics)]], a fictional character from Marvel Comics * One of the twins [[Nara-Narayana]] in Hindu mythology * "Nara", song from the album ''Unearthed'' by [[E.S. Posthumus]] * [[Shikamaru Nara]], fictional character in the manga and anime series ''Naruto'' * "Nara", "Arrival In Nara" and "Leaving Nara", songs from the 2014 album ''[[This Is All Yours]]'' by alt-J
== Other uses == * [[Nara Hotel]], a hotel in Nara, Japan * [[Nara Station]], a railway station in Nara, Japan * [[Nara Television]], a Japanese television and radio broadcaster headquartered in Nara, Japan * [[Nara University]], a private university in Misasagi-cho, Nara, Japan * [[Nara Women's University]], a national university in Nara, Japan * [[Nara period]], period of Japanese history, named after the former capital city * [[Japanese ship Nara|Japanese ship ''Nara'']], {{Nihongo||楢 / なら||"oak"}}, several Japanese ships * [[Nara plant]] (''[[Acanthosicyos horridus]]''), a melon species of the Namib desert
== NARA as an acronym == '''NARA''' may stand for: * [[National Alien Registration Authority]], a defunct agency of the Government of Pakistan * [[National Amateur Rowing Association]], the governing body for rowing by working men in Britain prior to 1956 * [[National Archives and Records Administration]], an agency of the U.S. government * [[Neale Analysis of Reading Ability]], an assessment of comprehension and word reading accuracy
==See also== * [[Narra (disambiguation)]] * [[Naro (disambiguation)]] * [[Nari (disambiguation)]]
{{Disambiguation|geo|given name|surname}}

revision.text:
{{wiktionary|Nara|nara}} '''Nara''' or '''Na-ra''' may refer to:
{{tocright}} == Places and jurisdictions == * [[Nara Prefecture]], a prefecture of Japan ** [[Nara (city)]], the capital city of Nara Prefecture, Japan * Nara, an ancient Roman city in Tunisia, now called [[Bir El Hafey]] * [[Nara, Attock]], a village in Attock, Pakistan * [[Nara, Jhelum]], a village in Jhelum, Pakistan * [[Nara Matore]], a village in Kahuta Tehsil, Rawalpindi District, Punjab, Pakistan * [[Nara, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa]], a Union Council of Abbottabad, Pakistan * [[Nara, Mali]], a town in Mali * [[Nara Basin]], a valley in Nara Prefecture, Japan * [[Nara Canal]], an excavated waterway in Sindh province, Pakistan * [[Nara Canal]], a paleochannel of the Indus and the Hakra, partly used by the Nara Canal * [[Nara (Oka)]], a river in Russia * [[Nara River (India)]], a river in Gujarat, India * [[Nara Burnu]], a cape in Turkey
== People and society == * [[Nara people]], a Nilotic ethnic minority inhabiting Eritrea * [[Nara language]], the mother tongue of the Nara people * [[Nara clan]], a Manchu clan in China * [[National Radical Camp]] or Nara Party, several Polish nationalist groups
== People with the name Nara == ===Given name=== * [[Nara Nath Acharya]] (1906–1988), Nepalese poet * [[Nara Apaya]], king of the Mrauk-U Dynasty of Arakan * [[Nara Bahadur Bista]], Nepalese politician * [[Nara Bahadur Dahal]] (born 1960), Nepalese long-distance runner * [[Nara Falcón]] (born 1981), Mexican synchronized swimmer * [[Jang Na-ra]] (born 1981), South Korean female singer * [[Nara Bahadur Karmacharya]], Nepalese politician * [[Nara Kollery]] (died 2015), Indian sound recordist * [[Kwon Nara]] (born 1991), South Korean female singer * [[Nara Leão]] (1942–1989), Brazilian female singer * [[Nara Lokesh]] (born 1983), Indian male politician * [[Nara Rohit]] (born 1984), Indian film actor and producer * [[Nara Bhupal Shah]] (1697–1743), king of the Gorkha state in Nepal
===Surname=== * [[Erika Nara]], Japanese Paralympic swimmer * [[Kurumi Nara]] (born 1991), Japanese tennis player * [[Rao Nara]] (1420–1487), crown Prince of Nadol, India *{{Nihongo|[[Setsuo Nara]]|奈良 節雄||born 1936}}, Japanese basketball player * [[Takeji Nara]] (1868–1962), general in the Imperial Japanese Army * [[Tatsuki Nara]] (born 1993), Japanese footballer * [[Tatunca Nara]] (born 1941), German-Brazilian jungle guide who invented the lost city of Akakor * [[Tōru Nara]], Japanese voice actor * [[Wanda Nara]] (born 1986), Argentine media personality and football agent * [[Yoshitomo Nara]] (born 1959), Japanese artist * [[Zaira Nara]] (born 1988), Argentine model and TV host * [[Empress Xiaojingxian|Ula Nara Duoqimuli]], Empress consort of Qing dynasty Yongzheng Emperor
== Arts and mythology == * [[Nara (Arjuna)]], Arjuna in his previous birth was Nara * ''[[Nara (album)]]'', by Emily Wurramara * [[Nara (comics)]], a fictional character from Marvel Comics * One of the twins [[Nara-Narayana]] in Hindu mythology * "Nara", song from the album ''Unearthed'' by [[E.S. Posthumus]] * [[Shikamaru Nara]], fictional character in the manga and anime series ''Naruto'' * "Nara", "Arrival In Nara" and "Leaving Nara", songs from the 2014 album ''[[This Is All Yours]]'' by alt-J
== Other uses == * [[Nara Hotel]], a hotel in Nara, Japan * [[Nara Station]], a railway station in Nara, Japan * [[Nara Television]], a Japanese television and radio broadcaster headquartered in Nara, Japan * [[Nara University]], a private university in Misasagi-cho, Nara, Japan * [[Nara Women's University]], a national university in Nara, Japan * [[Nara period]], period of Japanese history, named after the former capital city * [[Japanese ship Nara|Japanese ship ''Nara'']], {{Nihongo||楢 / なら||"oak"}}, several Japanese ships * [[Nara plant]] (''[[Acanthosicyos horridus]]''), a melon species of the Namib desert
== NARA as an acronym == '''NARA''' may stand for: * [[National Alien Registration Authority]], a defunct agency of the Government of Pakistan * [[National Amateur Rowing Association]], the governing body for rowing by working men in Britain prior to 1956 * [[National Archives and Records Administration]], an agency of the U.S. government * [[Neale Analysis of Reading Ability]], an assessment of comprehension and word reading accuracy
==See also== * [[Narra (disambiguation)]] * [[Naro (disambiguation)]] * [[Nari (disambiguation)]]
{{Disambiguation|geo|given name|surname}}

== People with the name Nara == ===Given name=== * [[Nara Nath Acharya]] (1906–1988), Nepalese poet * [[Nara Apaya]], king of the Mrauk-U Dynasty of Arakan * [[Nara Bahadur Bista]], Nepalese politician * [[Nara Bahadur Dahal]] (born 1960), Nepalese long-distance runner * [[Nara Falcón]] (born 1981), Mexican synchronized swimmer * [[:en:Jang Na-ra|Jang Na-ra]] (born 1981), South Korean female singer * [[Nara Bahadur Karmacharya]], Nepalese politician * [[Nara Kollery]] (died 2015), Indian sound recordist * [[:en:Kwon Nara|Kwon Nara]] (born 1991), South Korean female singer * [[Nara Leão]] (1942–1989), Brazilian female singer * [[Nara Lokesh]] (born 1983), Indian male politician * [[Nara Rohit]] (born 1984), Indian film actor and producer * [[Nara Bhupal Shah]] (1697–1743), king of the Gorkha state in Nepal
===Surname=== * [[:en:Erika Nara|Erika Nara]], Japanese Paralympic swimmer * [[:en:Kurumi Nara|Kurumi Nara]] (born 1991), Japanese tennis player * [[Rao Nara]] (1420–1487), crown Prince of Nadol, India *{{Nihongo|[[Setsuo Nara]]|奈良 節雄||born 1936}}, Japanese basketball player * [[Takeji Nara]] (1868–1962), general in the Imperial Japanese Army * [[Tatsuki Nara]] (born 1993), Japanese footballer * [[Tatunca Nara]] (born 1941), German-Brazilian jungle guide who invented the lost city of Akakor * [[Tōru Nara]], Japanese voice actor * [[Wanda Nara]] (born 1986), Argentine media personality and football agent * [[Yoshitomo Nara]] (born 1959), Japanese artist * [[Zaira Nara]] (born 1988), Argentine model and TV host * [[:en:Empress Xiaojingxian|Ula Nara Duoqimuli]], Empress consort of Qing dynasty Yongzheng Emperor
== Arts and mythology == * [[Nara (Arjuna)]], Arjuna in his previous birth was Nara * ''[[Nara (album)]]'', by Emily Wurramara * [[Nara (comics)]], a fictional character from Marvel Comics * One of the twins [[Nara-Narayana]] in Hindu mythology * "Nara", song from the album ''Unearthed'' by [[:en:E.S. Posthumus|E.S. Posthumus]] * [[Shikamaru Nara]], fictional character in the manga and anime series ''Naruto'' * "Nara", "Arrival In Nara" and "Leaving Nara", songs from the 2014 album ''[[This Is All Yours]]'' by alt-J
== Other uses == * [[Nara Hotel]], a hotel in Nara, Japan * [[Nara Station]], a railway station in Nara, Japan * [[Nara Television]], a Japanese television and radio broadcaster headquartered in Nara, Japan * [[Nara University]], a private university in Misasagi-cho, Nara, Japan * [[Nara Women's University]], a national university in Nara, Japan * [[Nara period]], period of Japanese history, named after the former capital city * [[:en:Japanese ship Nara|Japanese ship ''Nara'']], {{Nihongo||楢 / なら||"oak"}}, several Japanese ships * [[Nara plant]] (''[[:en:Acanthosicyos horridus|Acanthosicyos horridus]]''), a melon species of the Namib desert
== NARA as an acronym == '''NARA''' may stand for: * [[National Alien Registration Authority]], a defunct agency of the Government of Pakistan * [[National Amateur Rowing Association]], the governing body for rowing by working men in Britain prior to 1956 * [[National Archives and Records Administration]], an agency of the U.S. government * [[Neale Analysis of Reading Ability]], an assessment of comprehension and word reading accuracy

{{wiktionary|Nara|nara}} '''Nara''' or '''Na-ra''' may refer to:
{{tocright}} == Places and jurisdictions == * [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], a prefecture of Japan ** [[Nara (city)]], the capital city of Nara Prefecture, Japan * Nara, an ancient Roman city in Tunisia, now called [[:en:Bir El Hafey|Bir El Hafey]] * [[:en:Nara, Attock|Nara, Attock]], a village in Attock, Pakistan * [[:en:Nara, Jhelum|Nara, Jhelum]], a village in Jhelum, Pakistan * [[Nara Matore]], a village in Kahuta Tehsil, Rawalpindi District, Punjab, Pakistan * [[:en:Nara, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa|Nara, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa]], a Union Council of Abbottabad, Pakistan * [[:en:Nara, Mali|Nara, Mali]], a town in Mali * [[Nara Basin]], a valley in Nara Prefecture, Japan * [[Nara Canal]], an excavated waterway in Sindh province, Pakistan * [[Nara Canal]], a paleochannel of the Indus and the Hakra, partly used by the Nara Canal * [[:en:Nara (Oka)|Nara (Oka)]], a river in Russia * [[Nara River (India)]], a river in Gujarat, India * [[Nara Burnu]], a cape in Turkey
== People and society == * [[Nara people]], a Nilotic ethnic minority inhabiting Eritrea * [[Nara language]], the mother tongue of the Nara people * [[Nara clan]], a Manchu clan in China * [[National Radical Camp]] or Nara Party, several Polish nationalist groups
== People with the name Nara == ===Given name=== * [[Nara Nath Acharya]] (1906–1988), Nepalese poet * [[Nara Apaya]], king of the Mrauk-U Dynasty of Arakan * [[Nara Bahadur Bista]], Nepalese politician * [[Nara Bahadur Dahal]] (born 1960), Nepalese long-distance runner * [[Nara Falcón]] (born 1981), Mexican synchronized swimmer * [[:en:Jang Na-ra|Jang Na-ra]] (born 1981), South Korean female singer * [[Nara Bahadur Karmacharya]], Nepalese politician * [[Nara Kollery]] (died 2015), Indian sound recordist * [[:en:Kwon Nara|Kwon Nara]] (born 1991), South Korean female singer * [[Nara Leão]] (1942–1989), Brazilian female singer * [[Nara Lokesh]] (born 1983), Indian male politician * [[Nara Rohit]] (born 1984), Indian film actor and producer * [[Nara Bhupal Shah]] (1697–1743), king of the Gorkha state in Nepal
===Surname=== * [[:en:Erika Nara|Erika Nara]], Japanese Paralympic swimmer * [[:en:Kurumi Nara|Kurumi Nara]] (born 1991), Japanese tennis player * [[Rao Nara]] (1420–1487), crown Prince of Nadol, India *{{Nihongo|[[Setsuo Nara]]|奈良 節雄||born 1936}}, Japanese basketball player * [[Takeji Nara]] (1868–1962), general in the Imperial Japanese Army * [[Tatsuki Nara]] (born 1993), Japanese footballer * [[Tatunca Nara]] (born 1941), German-Brazilian jungle guide who invented the lost city of Akakor * [[Tōru Nara]], Japanese voice actor * [[Wanda Nara]] (born 1986), Argentine media personality and football agent * [[Yoshitomo Nara]] (born 1959), Japanese artist * [[Zaira Nara]] (born 1988), Argentine model and TV host * [[:en:Empress Xiaojingxian|Ula Nara Duoqimuli]], Empress consort of Qing dynasty Yongzheng Emperor
== Arts and mythology == * [[:en:Nara (Arjuna)|Nara (Arjuna)]], Arjuna in his previous birth was Nara * ''[[:en:Nara (album)|Nara (album)]]'', by Emily Wurramara * [[Nara (comics)]], a fictional character from Marvel Comics * One of the twins [[:en:Nara-Narayana|Nara-Narayana]] in Hindu mythology * "Nara", song from the album ''Unearthed'' by [[:en:E.S. Posthumus|E.S. Posthumus]] * [[Shikamaru Nara]], fictional character in the manga and anime series ''Naruto'' * "Nara", "Arrival In Nara" and "Leaving Nara", songs from the 2014 album ''[[This Is All Yours]]'' by alt-J
== Other uses == * [[Nara Hotel]], a hotel in Nara, Japan * [[Nara Station]], a railway station in Nara, Japan * [[Nara Television]], a Japanese television and radio broadcaster headquartered in Nara, Japan * [[Nara University]], a private university in Misasagi-cho, Nara, Japan * [[Nara Women's University]], a national university in Nara, Japan * [[Nara period]], period of Japanese history, named after the former capital city * [[:en:Japanese ship Nara|Japanese ship ''Nara'']], {{Nihongo||楢 / なら||"oak"}}, several Japanese ships * [[Nara plant]] (''[[:en:Acanthosicyos horridus|Acanthosicyos horridus]]''), a melon species of the Namib desert
== NARA as an acronym == '''NARA''' may stand for: * [[National Alien Registration Authority]], a defunct agency of the Government of Pakistan * [[National Amateur Rowing Association]], the governing body for rowing by working men in Britain prior to 1956 * [[National Archives and Records Administration]], an agency of the U.S. government * [[Neale Analysis of Reading Ability]], an assessment of comprehension and word reading accuracy
==See also== * [[Narra (disambiguation)]] * [[Naro (disambiguation)]] * [[Nari (disambiguation)]]
{{Disambiguation|geo|given name|surname}}

revision.text:
{{wiktionary|Nara|nara}} '''Nara''' or '''Na-ra''' may refer to:
{{tocright}} == Places and jurisdictions == * [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], a prefecture of Japan ** [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara (city)]], the capital city of Nara Prefecture, Japan * Nara, an ancient Roman city in Tunisia, now called [[:en:Bir El Hafey|Bir El Hafey]] * [[:en:Nara, Attock|Nara, Attock]], a village in Attock, Pakistan * [[:en:Nara, Jhelum|Nara, Jhelum]], a village in Jhelum, Pakistan * [[Nara Matore]], a village in Kahuta Tehsil, Rawalpindi District, Punjab, Pakistan * [[:en:Nara, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa|Nara, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa]], a Union Council of Abbottabad, Pakistan * [[:en:Nara, Mali|Nara, Mali]], a town in Mali * [[Nara Basin]], a valley in Nara Prefecture, Japan * [[Nara Canal]], an excavated waterway in Sindh province, Pakistan * [[Nara Canal]], a paleochannel of the Indus and the Hakra, partly used by the Nara Canal * [[:en:Nara (Oka)|Nara (Oka)]], a river in Russia * [[Nara River (India)]], a river in Gujarat, India * [[Nara Burnu]], a cape in Turkey
== People and society == * [[Nara people]], a Nilotic ethnic minority inhabiting Eritrea * [[Nara language]], the mother tongue of the Nara people * [[Nara clan]], a Manchu clan in China * [[National Radical Camp]] or Nara Party, several Polish nationalist groups
== People with the name Nara == ===Given name=== * [[Nara Nath Acharya]] (1906–1988), Nepalese poet * [[:en:Nara Apaya|Nara Apaya]], king of the Mrauk-U Dynasty of Arakan * [[:en:Nara Bahadur Bista|Nara Bahadur Bista]], Nepalese politician * [[Nara Bahadur Dahal]] (born 1960), Nepalese long-distance runner * [[Nara Falcón]] (born 1981), Mexican synchronized swimmer * [[:en:Jang Na-ra|Jang Na-ra]] (born 1981), South Korean female singer * [[Nara Bahadur Karmacharya]], Nepalese politician * [[Nara Kollery]] (died 2015), Indian sound recordist * [[:en:Kwon Nara|Kwon Nara]] (born 1991), South Korean female singer * [[Nara Leão]] (1942–1989), Brazilian female singer * [[Nara Lokesh]] (born 1983), Indian male politician * [[Nara Rohit]] (born 1984), Indian film actor and producer * [[Nara Bhupal Shah]] (1697–1743), king of the Gorkha state in Nepal
===Surname=== * [[:en:Erika Nara|Erika Nara]], Japanese Paralympic swimmer * [[:en:Kurumi Nara|Kurumi Nara]] (born 1991), Japanese tennis player * [[Rao Nara]] (1420–1487), crown Prince of Nadol, India *{{Nihongo|[[Setsuo Nara]]|奈良 節雄||born 1936}}, Japanese basketball player * [[Takeji Nara]] (1868–1962), general in the Imperial Japanese Army * [[Tatsuki Nara]] (born 1993), Japanese footballer * [[Tatunca Nara]] (born 1941), German-Brazilian jungle guide who invented the lost city of Akakor * [[Tōru Nara]], Japanese voice actor * [[Wanda Nara]] (born 1986), Argentine media personality and football agent * [[Yoshitomo Nara]] (born 1959), Japanese artist * [[Zaira Nara]] (born 1988), Argentine model and TV host * [[:en:Empress Xiaojingxian|Ula Nara Duoqimuli]], Empress consort of Qing dynasty Yongzheng Emperor
== Arts and mythology == * [[:en:Nara (Arjuna)|Nara (Arjuna)]], Arjuna in his previous birth was Nara * ''[[:en:Nara (album)|Nara (album)]]'', by Emily Wurramara * [[:en:Nara (comics)|Nara (comics)]], a fictional character from Marvel Comics * One of the twins [[:en:Nara-Narayana|Nara-Narayana]] in Hindu mythology * "Nara", song from the album ''Unearthed'' by [[:en:E.S. Posthumus|E.S. Posthumus]] * [[Shikamaru Nara]], fictional character in the manga and anime series ''Naruto'' * "Nara", "Arrival In Nara" and "Leaving Nara", songs from the 2014 album ''[[This Is All Yours]]'' by alt-J
== Other uses == * [[Nara Hotel]], a hotel in Nara, Japan * [[Nara Station]], a railway station in Nara, Japan * [[Nara Television]], a Japanese television and radio broadcaster headquartered in Nara, Japan * [[Nara University]], a private university in Misasagi-cho, Nara, Japan * [[Nara Women's University]], a national university in Nara, Japan * [[Nara period]], period of Japanese history, named after the former capital city * [[:en:Japanese ship Nara|Japanese ship ''Nara'']], {{Nihongo||楢 / なら||"oak"}}, several Japanese ships * [[Nara plant]] (''[[:en:Acanthosicyos horridus|Acanthosicyos horridus]]''), a melon species of the Namib desert
== NARA as an acronym == '''NARA''' may stand for: * [[National Alien Registration Authority]], a defunct agency of the Government of Pakistan * [[National Amateur Rowing Association]], the governing body for rowing by working men in Britain prior to 1956 * [[National Archives and Records Administration]], an agency of the U.S. government * [[Neale Analysis of Reading Ability]], an assessment of comprehension and word reading accuracy
==See also== * [[Narra (disambiguation)]] * [[Naro (disambiguation)]] * [[Nari (disambiguation)]]
{{Disambiguation|geo|given name|surname}}

[[Category:All disambiguation pages]]
}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{wiktionary|Nara|nara}} '''Nara''' or '''Na-ra''' may refer to:
{{tocright}} == Places and jurisdictions == * [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], a prefecture of Japan ** [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara (city)]], the capital city of Nara Prefecture, Japan * Nara, an ancient Roman city in Tunisia, now called [[:en:Bir El Hafey|Bir El Hafey]] * [[:en:Nara, Attock|Nara, Attock]], a village in Attock, Pakistan * [[:en:Nara, Jhelum|Nara, Jhelum]], a village in Jhelum, Pakistan * [[Nara Matore]], a village in Kahuta Tehsil, Rawalpindi District, Punjab, Pakistan * [[:en:Nara, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa|Nara, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa]], a Union Council of Abbottabad, Pakistan * [[:en:Nara, Mali|Nara, Mali]], a town in Mali * [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], a valley in Nara Prefecture, Japan * [[Nara Canal]], an excavated waterway in Sindh province, Pakistan * [[Nara Canal]], a paleochannel of the Indus and the Hakra, partly used by the Nara Canal * [[:en:Nara (Oka)|Nara (Oka)]], a river in Russia * [[Nara River (India)]], a river in Gujarat, India * [[Nara Burnu]], a cape in Turkey
== People and society == * [[Nara people]], a Nilotic ethnic minority inhabiting Eritrea * [[Nara language]], the mother tongue of the Nara people * [[Nara clan]], a Manchu clan in China * [[National Radical Camp]] or Nara Party, several Polish nationalist groups
== People with the name Nara == ===Given name=== * [[Nara Nath Acharya]] (1906–1988), Nepalese poet * [[:en:Nara Apaya|Nara Apaya]], king of the Mrauk-U Dynasty of Arakan * [[:en:Nara Bahadur Bista|Nara Bahadur Bista]], Nepalese politician * [[:en:Nara Bahadur Dahal|Nara Bahadur Dahal]] (born 1960), Nepalese long-distance runner * [[Nara Falcón]] (born 1981), Mexican synchronized swimmer * [[:en:Jang Na-ra|Jang Na-ra]] (born 1981), South Korean female singer * [[:en:Nara Bahadur Karmacharya|Nara Bahadur Karmacharya]], Nepalese politician * [[Nara Kollery]] (died 2015), Indian sound recordist * [[:en:Kwon Nara|Kwon Nara]] (born 1991), South Korean female singer * [[Nara Leão]] (1942–1989), Brazilian female singer * [[Nara Lokesh]] (born 1983), Indian male politician * [[Nara Rohit]] (born 1984), Indian film actor and producer * [[:en:Nara Bhupal Shah|Nara Bhupal Shah]] (1697–1743), king of the Gorkha state in Nepal
===Surname=== * [[:en:Erika Nara|Erika Nara]], Japanese Paralympic swimmer * [[:en:Kurumi Nara|Kurumi Nara]] (born 1991), Japanese tennis player * [[Rao Nara]] (1420–1487), crown Prince of Nadol, India *{{Nihongo|[[Setsuo Nara]]|奈良 節雄||born 1936}}, Japanese basketball player * [[Takeji Nara]] (1868–1962), general in the Imperial Japanese Army * [[Tatsuki Nara]] (born 1993), Japanese footballer * [[Tatunca Nara]] (born 1941), German-Brazilian jungle guide who invented the lost city of Akakor * [[Tōru Nara]], Japanese voice actor * [[Wanda Nara]] (born 1986), Argentine media personality and football agent * [[Yoshitomo Nara]] (born 1959), Japanese artist * [[Zaira Nara]] (born 1988), Argentine model and TV host * [[:en:Empress Xiaojingxian|Ula Nara Duoqimuli]], Empress consort of Qing dynasty Yongzheng Emperor
== Arts and mythology == * [[:en:Nara (Arjuna)|Nara (Arjuna)]], Arjuna in his previous birth was Nara * ''[[:en:Nara (album)|Nara (album)]]'', by Emily Wurramara * [[:en:Nara (comics)|Nara (comics)]], a fictional character from Marvel Comics * One of the twins [[:en:Nara-Narayana|Nara-Narayana]] in Hindu mythology * "Nara", song from the album ''Unearthed'' by [[:en:E.S. Posthumus|E.S. Posthumus]] * [[Shikamaru Nara]], fictional character in the manga and anime series ''Naruto'' * "Nara", "Arrival In Nara" and "Leaving Nara", songs from the 2014 album ''[[This Is All Yours]]'' by alt-J
== Other uses == * [[Nara Hotel]], a hotel in Nara, Japan * [[Nara Station]], a railway station in Nara, Japan * [[Nara Television]], a Japanese television and radio broadcaster headquartered in Nara, Japan * [[Nara University]], a private university in Misasagi-cho, Nara, Japan * [[Nara Women's University]], a national university in Nara, Japan * [[Nara period]], period of Japanese history, named after the former capital city * [[:en:Japanese ship Nara|Japanese ship ''Nara'']], {{Nihongo||楢 / なら||"oak"}}, several Japanese ships * [[Nara plant]] (''[[:en:Acanthosicyos horridus|Acanthosicyos horridus]]''), a melon species of the Namib desert
== NARA as an acronym == '''NARA''' may stand for: * [[National Alien Registration Authority]], a defunct agency of the Government of Pakistan * [[National Amateur Rowing Association]], the governing body for rowing by working men in Britain prior to 1956 * [[National Archives and Records Administration]], an agency of the U.S. government * [[Neale Analysis of Reading Ability]], an assessment of comprehension and word reading accuracy
==See also== * [[Narra (disambiguation)]] * [[Naro (disambiguation)]] * [[Nari (disambiguation)]]

revision.text:
{{wiktionary|Nara|nara}} '''Nara''' or '''Na-ra''' may refer to:
{{tocright}} == Places and jurisdictions == * [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], a prefecture of Japan ** [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara (city)]], the capital city of Nara Prefecture, Japan * Nara, an ancient Roman city in Tunisia, now called [[:en:Bir El Hafey|Bir El Hafey]] * [[:en:Nara, Attock|Nara, Attock]], a village in Attock, Pakistan * [[:en:Nara, Jhelum|Nara, Jhelum]], a village in Jhelum, Pakistan * [[:en:Nara Matore|Nara Matore]], a village in Kahuta Tehsil, Rawalpindi District, Punjab, Pakistan * [[:en:Nara, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa|Nara, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa]], a Union Council of Abbottabad, Pakistan * [[:en:Nara, Mali|Nara, Mali]], a town in Mali * [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], a valley in Nara Prefecture, Japan * [[:en:Nara Canal|Nara Canal]], an excavated waterway in Sindh province, Pakistan * [[:en:Nara Canal|Nara Canal]], a paleochannel of the Indus and the Hakra, partly used by the Nara Canal * [[:en:Nara (Oka)|Nara (Oka)]], a river in Russia * [[:en:Nara River (India)|Nara River (India)]], a river in Gujarat, India * [[:en:Nara Burnu|Nara Burnu]], a cape in Turkey
== People and society == * [[Nara people]], a Nilotic ethnic minority inhabiting Eritrea * [[Nara language]], the mother tongue of the Nara people * [[Nara clan]], a Manchu clan in China * [[National Radical Camp]] or Nara Party, several Polish nationalist groups
== People with the name Nara == ===Given name=== * [[:en:Nara Nath Acharya|Nara Nath Acharya]] (1906–1988), Nepalese poet * [[:en:Nara Apaya|Nara Apaya]], king of the Mrauk-U Dynasty of Arakan * [[:en:Nara Bahadur Bista|Nara Bahadur Bista]], Nepalese politician * [[:en:Nara Bahadur Dahal|Nara Bahadur Dahal]] (born 1960), Nepalese long-distance runner * [[:en:Nara Falcón|Nara Falcón]] (born 1981), Mexican synchronized swimmer * [[:en:Jang Na-ra|Jang Na-ra]] (born 1981), South Korean female singer * [[:en:Nara Bahadur Karmacharya|Nara Bahadur Karmacharya]], Nepalese politician * [[:en:Nara Kollery|Nara Kollery]] (died 2015), Indian sound recordist * [[:en:Kwon Nara|Kwon Nara]] (born 1991), South Korean female singer * [[:en:Nara Leão|Nara Leão]] (1942–1989), Brazilian female singer * [[:en:Nara Lokesh|Nara Lokesh]] (born 1983), Indian male politician * [[:en:Nara Rohit|Nara Rohit]] (born 1984), Indian film actor and producer * [[:en:Nara Bhupal Shah|Nara Bhupal Shah]] (1697–1743), king of the Gorkha state in Nepal
===Surname=== * [[:en:Erika Nara|Erika Nara]], Japanese Paralympic swimmer * [[:en:Kurumi Nara|Kurumi Nara]] (born 1991), Japanese tennis player * [[Rao Nara]] (1420–1487), crown Prince of Nadol, India *{{Nihongo|[[Setsuo Nara]]|奈良 節雄||born 1936}}, Japanese basketball player * [[Takeji Nara]] (1868–1962), general in the Imperial Japanese Army * [[Tatsuki Nara]] (born 1993), Japanese footballer * [[Tatunca Nara]] (born 1941), German-Brazilian jungle guide who invented the lost city of Akakor * [[Tōru Nara]], Japanese voice actor * [[Wanda Nara]] (born 1986), Argentine media personality and football agent * [[Yoshitomo Nara]] (born 1959), Japanese artist * [[Zaira Nara]] (born 1988), Argentine model and TV host * [[:en:Empress Xiaojingxian|Ula Nara Duoqimuli]], Empress consort of Qing dynasty Yongzheng Emperor
== Arts and mythology == * [[:en:Nara (Arjuna)|Nara (Arjuna)]], Arjuna in his previous birth was Nara * ''[[:en:Nara (album)|Nara (album)]]'', by Emily Wurramara * [[:en:Nara (comics)|Nara (comics)]], a fictional character from Marvel Comics * One of the twins [[:en:Nara-Narayana|Nara-Narayana]] in Hindu mythology * "Nara", song from the album ''Unearthed'' by [[:en:E.S. Posthumus|E.S. Posthumus]] * [[Shikamaru Nara]], fictional character in the manga and anime series ''Naruto'' * "Nara", "Arrival In Nara" and "Leaving Nara", songs from the 2014 album ''[[This Is All Yours]]'' by alt-J
== Other uses == * [[:en:Nara Hotel|Nara Hotel]], a hotel in Nara, Japan * [[Nara Station]], a railway station in Nara, Japan * [[Nara Television]], a Japanese television and radio broadcaster headquartered in Nara, Japan * [[Nara University]], a private university in Misasagi-cho, Nara, Japan * [[Nara Women's University]], a national university in Nara, Japan * [[Nara period]], period of Japanese history, named after the former capital city * [[:en:Japanese ship Nara|Japanese ship ''Nara'']], {{Nihongo||楢 / なら||"oak"}}, several Japanese ships * [[Nara plant]] (''[[:en:Acanthosicyos horridus|Acanthosicyos horridus]]''), a melon species of the Namib desert
== NARA as an acronym == '''NARA''' may stand for: * [[National Alien Registration Authority]], a defunct agency of the Government of Pakistan * [[National Amateur Rowing Association]], the governing body for rowing by working men in Britain prior to 1956 * [[National Archives and Records Administration]], an agency of the U.S. government * [[Neale Analysis of Reading Ability]], an assessment of comprehension and word reading accuracy
==See also== * [[Narra (disambiguation)]] * [[Naro (disambiguation)]] * [[Nari (disambiguation)]]

revision.text:
{{wiktionary|Nara|nara}} '''Nara''' or '''Na-ra''' may refer to:
{{tocright}} == Places and jurisdictions == * [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], a prefecture of Japan ** [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara (city)]], the capital city of Nara Prefecture, Japan * Nara, an ancient Roman city in Tunisia, now called [[:en:Bir El Hafey|Bir El Hafey]] * [[:en:Nara, Attock|Nara, Attock]], a village in Attock, Pakistan * [[:en:Nara, Jhelum|Nara, Jhelum]], a village in Jhelum, Pakistan * [[:en:Nara Matore|Nara Matore]], a village in Kahuta Tehsil, Rawalpindi District, Punjab, Pakistan * [[:en:Nara, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa|Nara, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa]], a Union Council of Abbottabad, Pakistan * [[:en:Nara, Mali|Nara, Mali]], a town in Mali * [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], a valley in Nara Prefecture, Japan * [[:en:Nara Canal|Nara Canal]], an excavated waterway in Sindh province, Pakistan * [[:en:Nara Canal|Nara Canal]], a paleochannel of the Indus and the Hakra, partly used by the Nara Canal * [[:en:Nara (Oka)|Nara (Oka)]], a river in Russia * [[:en:Nara River (India)|Nara River (India)]], a river in Gujarat, India * [[:en:Nara Burnu|Nara Burnu]], a cape in Turkey
== People and society == * [[:en:Nara people|Nara people]], a Nilotic ethnic minority inhabiting Eritrea * [[:en:Nara language|Nara language]], the mother tongue of the Nara people * [[:en:Nara clan|Nara clan]], a Manchu clan in China * [[National Radical Camp]] or Nara Party, several Polish nationalist groups
== People with the name Nara == ===Given name=== * [[:en:Nara Nath Acharya|Nara Nath Acharya]] (1906–1988), Nepalese poet * [[:en:Nara Apaya|Nara Apaya]], king of the Mrauk-U Dynasty of Arakan * [[:en:Nara Bahadur Bista|Nara Bahadur Bista]], Nepalese politician * [[:en:Nara Bahadur Dahal|Nara Bahadur Dahal]] (born 1960), Nepalese long-distance runner * [[:en:Nara Falcón|Nara Falcón]] (born 1981), Mexican synchronized swimmer * [[:en:Jang Na-ra|Jang Na-ra]] (born 1981), South Korean female singer * [[:en:Nara Bahadur Karmacharya|Nara Bahadur Karmacharya]], Nepalese politician * [[:en:Nara Kollery|Nara Kollery]] (died 2015), Indian sound recordist * [[:en:Kwon Nara|Kwon Nara]] (born 1991), South Korean female singer * [[:en:Nara Leão|Nara Leão]] (1942–1989), Brazilian female singer * [[:en:Nara Lokesh|Nara Lokesh]] (born 1983), Indian male politician * [[:en:Nara Rohit|Nara Rohit]] (born 1984), Indian film actor and producer * [[:en:Nara Bhupal Shah|Nara Bhupal Shah]] (1697–1743), king of the Gorkha state in Nepal
===Surname=== * [[:en:Erika Nara|Erika Nara]], Japanese Paralympic swimmer * [[:en:Kurumi Nara|Kurumi Nara]] (born 1991), Japanese tennis player * [[Rao Nara]] (1420–1487), crown Prince of Nadol, India *{{Nihongo|[[Setsuo Nara]]|奈良 節雄||born 1936}}, Japanese basketball player * [[Takeji Nara]] (1868–1962), general in the Imperial Japanese Army * [[Tatsuki Nara]] (born 1993), Japanese footballer * [[Tatunca Nara]] (born 1941), German-Brazilian jungle guide who invented the lost city of Akakor * [[Tōru Nara]], Japanese voice actor * [[Wanda Nara]] (born 1986), Argentine media personality and football agent * [[Yoshitomo Nara]] (born 1959), Japanese artist * [[Zaira Nara]] (born 1988), Argentine model and TV host * [[:en:Empress Xiaojingxian|Ula Nara Duoqimuli]], Empress consort of Qing dynasty Yongzheng Emperor
== Arts and mythology == * [[:en:Nara (Arjuna)|Nara (Arjuna)]], Arjuna in his previous birth was Nara * ''[[:en:Nara (album)|Nara (album)]]'', by Emily Wurramara * [[:en:Nara (comics)|Nara (comics)]], a fictional character from Marvel Comics * One of the twins [[:en:Nara-Narayana|Nara-Narayana]] in Hindu mythology * "Nara", song from the album ''Unearthed'' by [[:en:E.S. Posthumus|E.S. Posthumus]] * [[Shikamaru Nara]], fictional character in the manga and anime series ''Naruto'' * "Nara", "Arrival In Nara" and "Leaving Nara", songs from the 2014 album ''[[This Is All Yours]]'' by alt-J
== Other uses == * [[:en:Nara Hotel|Nara Hotel]], a hotel in Nara, Japan * [[:en:Nara Station|Nara Station]], a railway station in Nara, Japan * [[:en:Nara Television|Nara Television]], a Japanese television and radio broadcaster headquartered in Nara, Japan * [[:en:Nara University|Nara University]], a private university in Misasagi-cho, Nara, Japan * [[:en:Nara Women's University|Nara Women's University]], a national university in Nara, Japan * [[Nara period]], period of Japanese history, named after the former capital city * [[:en:Japanese ship Nara|Japanese ship ''Nara'']], {{Nihongo||楢 / なら||"oak"}}, several Japanese ships * [[Nara plant]] (''[[:en:Acanthosicyos horridus|Acanthosicyos horridus]]''), a melon species of the Namib desert
== NARA as an acronym == '''NARA''' may stand for: * [[National Alien Registration Authority]], a defunct agency of the Government of Pakistan * [[National Amateur Rowing Association]], the governing body for rowing by working men in Britain prior to 1956 * [[National Archives and Records Administration]], an agency of the U.S. government * [[Neale Analysis of Reading Ability]], an assessment of comprehension and word reading accuracy
==See also== * [[Narra (disambiguation)]] * [[Naro (disambiguation)]] * [[Nari (disambiguation)]]

revision.text:
{{wiktionary|Nara|nara}} '''Nara''' or '''Na-ra''' may refer to:
{{tocright}} == Places and jurisdictions == * [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], a prefecture of Japan ** [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara (city)]], the capital city of Nara Prefecture, Japan * Nara, an ancient Roman city in Tunisia, now called [[:en:Bir El Hafey|Bir El Hafey]] * [[:en:Nara, Attock|Nara, Attock]], a village in Attock, Pakistan * [[:en:Nara, Jhelum|Nara, Jhelum]], a village in Jhelum, Pakistan * [[:en:Nara Matore|Nara Matore]], a village in Kahuta Tehsil, Rawalpindi District, Punjab, Pakistan * [[:en:Nara, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa|Nara, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa]], a Union Council of Abbottabad, Pakistan * [[:en:Nara, Mali|Nara, Mali]], a town in Mali * [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], a valley in Nara Prefecture, Japan * [[:en:Nara Canal|Nara Canal]], an excavated waterway in Sindh province, Pakistan * [[:en:Nara Canal|Nara Canal]], a paleochannel of the Indus and the Hakra, partly used by the Nara Canal * [[:en:Nara (Oka)|Nara (Oka)]], a river in Russia * [[:en:Nara River (India)|Nara River (India)]], a river in Gujarat, India * [[:en:Nara Burnu|Nara Burnu]], a cape in Turkey
== People and society == * [[:en:Nara people|Nara people]], a Nilotic ethnic minority inhabiting Eritrea * [[:en:Nara language|Nara language]], the mother tongue of the Nara people * [[:en:Nara clan|Nara clan]], a Manchu clan in China * [[National Radical Camp]] or Nara Party, several Polish nationalist groups
== People with the name Nara == ===Given name=== * [[:en:Nara Nath Acharya|Nara Nath Acharya]] (1906–1988), Nepalese poet * [[:en:Nara Apaya|Nara Apaya]], king of the Mrauk-U Dynasty of Arakan * [[:en:Nara Bahadur Bista|Nara Bahadur Bista]], Nepalese politician * [[:en:Nara Bahadur Dahal|Nara Bahadur Dahal]] (born 1960), Nepalese long-distance runner * [[:en:Nara Falcón|Nara Falcón]] (born 1981), Mexican synchronized swimmer * [[:en:Jang Na-ra|Jang Na-ra]] (born 1981), South Korean female singer * [[:en:Nara Bahadur Karmacharya|Nara Bahadur Karmacharya]], Nepalese politician * [[:en:Nara Kollery|Nara Kollery]] (died 2015), Indian sound recordist * [[:en:Kwon Nara|Kwon Nara]] (born 1991), South Korean female singer * [[:en:Nara Leão|Nara Leão]] (1942–1989), Brazilian female singer * [[:en:Nara Lokesh|Nara Lokesh]] (born 1983), Indian male politician * [[:en:Nara Rohit|Nara Rohit]] (born 1984), Indian film actor and producer * [[:en:Nara Bhupal Shah|Nara Bhupal Shah]] (1697–1743), king of the Gorkha state in Nepal
===Surname=== * [[:en:Erika Nara|Erika Nara]], Japanese Paralympic swimmer * [[:en:Kurumi Nara|Kurumi Nara]] (born 1991), Japanese tennis player * [[Rao Nara]] (1420–1487), crown Prince of Nadol, India *{{Nihongo|[[Setsuo Nara]]|奈良 節雄||born 1936}}, Japanese basketball player * [[Takeji Nara]] (1868–1962), general in the Imperial Japanese Army * [[Tatsuki Nara]] (born 1993), Japanese footballer * [[Tatunca Nara]] (born 1941), German-Brazilian jungle guide who invented the lost city of Akakor * [[Tōru Nara]], Japanese voice actor * [[Wanda Nara]] (born 1986), Argentine media personality and football agent * [[Yoshitomo Nara]] (born 1959), Japanese artist * [[Zaira Nara]] (born 1988), Argentine model and TV host * [[:en:Empress Xiaojingxian|Ula Nara Duoqimuli]], Empress consort of Qing dynasty Yongzheng Emperor
== Arts and mythology == * [[:en:Nara (Arjuna)|Nara (Arjuna)]], Arjuna in his previous birth was Nara * ''[[:en:Nara (album)|Nara (album)]]'', by Emily Wurramara * [[:en:Nara (comics)|Nara (comics)]], a fictional character from Marvel Comics * One of the twins [[:en:Nara-Narayana|Nara-Narayana]] in Hindu mythology * "Nara", song from the album ''Unearthed'' by [[:en:E.S. Posthumus|E.S. Posthumus]] * [[Shikamaru Nara]], fictional character in the manga and anime series ''Naruto'' * "Nara", "Arrival In Nara" and "Leaving Nara", songs from the 2014 album ''[[This Is All Yours]]'' by alt-J
== Other uses == * [[:en:Nara Hotel|Nara Hotel]], a hotel in Nara, Japan * [[:en:Nara Station|Nara Station]], a railway station in Nara, Japan * [[:en:Nara Television|Nara Television]], a Japanese television and radio broadcaster headquartered in Nara, Japan * [[:en:Nara University|Nara University]], a private university in Misasagi-cho, Nara, Japan * [[:en:Nara Women's University|Nara Women's University]], a national university in Nara, Japan * [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], period of Japanese history, named after the former capital city * [[:en:Japanese ship Nara|Japanese ship ''Nara'']], {{Nihongo||楢 / なら||"oak"}}, several Japanese ships * [[:en:Nara plant|Nara plant]] (''[[:en:Acanthosicyos horridus|Acanthosicyos horridus]]''), a melon species of the Namib desert
== NARA as an acronym == '''NARA''' may stand for: * [[:en:National Alien Registration Authority|National Alien Registration Authority]], a defunct agency of the Government of Pakistan * [[:en:National Amateur Rowing Association|National Amateur Rowing Association]], the governing body for rowing by working men in Britain prior to 1956 * [[National Archives and Records Administration]], an agency of the U.S. government * [[Neale Analysis of Reading Ability]], an assessment of comprehension and word reading accuracy
==See also== * [[:en:Narra (disambiguation)|Narra (disambiguation)]] * [[:en:Naro (disambiguation)|Naro (disambiguation)]] * [[:en:Nari (disambiguation)|Nari (disambiguation)]]

== People and society == * [[:en:Nara people|Nara people]], a Nilotic ethnic minority inhabiting Eritrea * [[:en:Nara language|Nara language]], the mother tongue of the Nara people * [[:en:Nara clan|Nara clan]], a Manchu clan in China * [[:en:National Radical Camp|National Radical Camp]] or Nara Party, several Polish nationalist groups
